flow.description: Flow: Apache Kafka with SASL authentication
flow.name: Apache Kafka with SASL authentication

parameters.description: One of: embedded-avro-schema / schema-reference-reader / schema-text-property
parameters.name: AVRO Schema Access Strategy

parameters.description: A comma-separated list of Kafka brokers to fetch data from. The same instance is used for the DLQ topic.
parameters.name: Kafka Bootstrap Servers

parameters.description: One of: PLAIN / SCRAM-SHA-256 / SCRAM-SHA-512
parameters.name: Kafka SASL Mechanism

parameters.description: The password to authenticate to Kafka
parameters.name: Kafka SASL Password

parameters.description: The username to authenticate to Kafka
parameters.name: Kafka SASL Username

parameters.description:
One of: SASL_PLAINTEXT / SASL_SSL
Other security protocols are not supported.
parameters.name: Kafka Security Protocol

parameters.description:
The method of authenticating to schema registry.
One of: NONE / BASIC
parameters.name: Schema Registry Authentication Type

parameters.description: The password for Schema Registry
parameters.name: Schema Registry Password

parameters.description: The URL of Schema Registry
parameters.name: Schema Registry URL

parameters.description: The username for Schema Registry
parameters.name: Schema Registry Username

parameters.description: The database where data will be persisted. It must already exist in Snowflake
parameters.name: Destination Database

parameters.description:
The schema where data will be persisted. It must already exist in Snowflake. This parameter is case sensitive.
Unless destination schema was created with double quoted name, this parameter should be provided in upper case
parameters.name: Destination Schema

parameters.description: Leave this blank when using Session Token for your Authentication Strategy. When using KEY_PAIR, provide your Snowflake account name formatted as [organization-name]-[account-name] where data will be persisted.
parameters.name: Snowflake Account Identifier

parameters.description: Using SNOWFLAKE_MANAGED will make use of the Snowflake managed token associated with the specified Snowflake Runtime Role. If using Openflow BYOC, it is also possible to use KEY_PAIR in order to specify a specific user and role via a cusom Key Pair.
parameters.name: Snowflake Authentication Strategy

parameters.description: Leave this blank when using Session Token for your Authentication Strategy. When using KEY_PAIR, provide the RSA private key used for authentication. The RSA key must be formatted according to PKCS8 standards and have standard PEM headers and footers. Note that either Snowflake Private Key File or Snowflake Private Key must be defined.
parameters.name: Snowflake Private Key

parameters.description: Leave this blank when using Session Token for your Authentication Strategy. When using KEY_PAIR, upload the file that contains the RSA Private Key used for authentication to Snowflake, formatted according to PKCS8 standards and having standard PEM headers and footers. The header line begins with -----BEGIN PRIVATE. Select the Reference asset checkbox to upload the private key file.
parameters.name: Snowflake Private Key File

parameters.description: Leave this blank when using Session Token for your Authentication Strategy. When using KEY_PAIR, provide the password associated with the Snowflake Private Key File.
parameters.name: Snowflake Private Key Password

parameters.description: When using Session Token for your Authentication Strategy, use your Runtime Role. You can find your Runtime Role in the Openflow UI, by going to View Details for your Runtime. When using Key Pair for your Authentication Strategy, use a valid role configured for your service user.
parameters.name: Snowflake Role

parameters.description: Leave this blank when using Session Token for your Authentication Strategy. When using KEY_PAIR, provide the user name used to connect to Snowflake instance.
parameters.name: Snowflake Username

parameters.description: Avro schema in case schema-text-property is used in AVRO Schema Access Strategy with the AVRO message format. Note: this should only be used in case all messages consumed from the configured Kafka Topic(s) share the same schema.
parameters.name: AVRO Schema

parameters.description: Specifies whether the processor ingests data into an Iceberg table. The processor fails if his property doesn’t match the actual table type.
parameters.name: Iceberg Enabled

parameters.description: One of: earliest / latest
parameters.name: Kafka Auto Offset Reset

parameters.description: DLQ topic to send messages with parsing errors to
parameters.name: Kafka DLQ Topic

parameters.description: The id of a consumer group used by the connector
parameters.name: Kafka Group Id

parameters.description:
One of: "names" / "pattern".
Specifies whether the “Kafka Topics” provided are a comma separated list of names or a single regular expression.
parameters.name: Kafka Topic Format

parameters.description: The name or pattern of the Kafka Topics from which the connector consumes data. More than one can be supplied if comma separated. See also "Kafka Topic Format" param.
parameters.name: Kafka Topics

parameters.description:
The format of messages in Kafka
One of: JSON / AVRO
parameters.name: Message Format

parameters.description:
Determines whether data is inserted into individual columns or a single RECORD_METADATA field.

One of: true / false
parameters.name: Schematization Enabled

parameters.description:
This optional parameter lets a user specify which topics should be mapped to which tables. Each topic and its table name should be separated by a colon (see example below). This table name must be a valid Snowflake unquoted identifier. The regular expressions cannot be ambiguous — any matched topic must match only a single target table. If empty or no matches found, topic name will be used as table name.

Examples: 'topic1:low_range,topic2:low_range,topic5:high_range,topic6:high_range' and 'topic[0-4]:low_range,topic[5-9]:high_range'
parameters.name: Topic To Table Map
